betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Machine Upgrade: Speed Upgrade§f

§aSpeed Upgrades reduce processing time for every NC machine.§f
The §bSpeed Upgrade§f (`nuclearcraft:upgrade:0`) is one of two §bMachine Upgrades§f in NuclearCraft. When inserted into an NC machine's §bupgrade slot§f, it reduces the machine's §bprocessing time§f per recipe by a fixed multiplier, allowing faster throughput at the cost of higher FE consumption.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:403)§f:
Per `addShapedOreRecipe(new ItemStack(NCItems.upgrade, 1, 0), ...)`:
- Output: §b1 × Speed Upgrade§f (`NCItems.upgrade`, meta 0)
- Pattern (3×3): §bL-R-L / R-P-R / L-R-L§f
  - §bL§f = §bLapis Lazuli§f (`gemLapis`)
  - §bR§f = §bRedstone Dust§f (`dustRedstone`)
  - §bP§f = §bHeavy Weighted Pressure Plate§f (`Blocks.HEAVY_WEIGHTED_PRESSURE_PLATE`)

§6Machine Upgrade Type (ItemUpgrade.java + MetaEnums.java:1379)§f:
Per `UpgradeType.SPEED("speed", 0)`:
- §bMeta value§f: 0 (Damage 0 in NBT)
- §bMax stack size§f: per `upgrade_stack_sizes[0]` (configurable, defaults to 8)
- §bInsertion§f: right-click the upgrade into an NC machine's GUI; or sneak-right-click on the machine block directly (per `ItemUpgrade.java:25`).

§6Effect on Machines§f:
When §bN§f Speed Upgrades are inserted into an NC machine:
- §bProcessing time§f is divided by a factor that scales with N (typically 1.5× speed per upgrade, capped at 8).
- §bFE consumption§f scales with the speedup — the machine consumes proportionally more power to maintain the higher throughput.
- §bHeat generation§f (for heat-generating machines) also scales with speedup.

§6Machines That Accept Speed Upgrades§f:
All §bUpgradableProcessor§f machines in NC:
- §bNuclear Furnace§f, §bAlloy Furnace§f
- §bIngot Former§f, §bPressurizer§f
- §bManufactory§f, §bAssembler§f
- §bChemical Reactor§f, §bFluid Infuser§f, §bFluid Extractor§f, §bFluid Enricher§f
- §bSalt Mixer§f, §bCrystallizer§f, §bMelter§f
- §bSupercooler§f, §bElectrolyzer§f
- §bCentrifuge§f, §bIsotope Separator§f, §bRock Crusher§f
- §bDecay Hastener§f, §bFission Irradiator§f, §bFuel Reprocessor§f

§6Max Upgrades Per Machine§f:
Most NC machines have 4-8 §bupgrade slots§f. The exact count is configured per-machine in the §b§f§cContainerUpgradableProcessor.java§f§f class (line 30-46 shows the upgrade slot logic).

§eTip§f: Speed Upgrades are §bcheap and stackable§f — always keep a §bchest of 64 Speed Upgrades§f next to your high-throughput machines (Assemblers, Manufactories, Chemical Reactors). Pair them with §bEnergy Upgrades§f to keep the FE cost manageable.
